issue.title: Lorebook recursive scan cannot activate entries across active lorebooks

coreClaim: Recursive lorebook scanning activates entries across the full active lorebook set from budget-selected frontier content while respecting depth and preventRecursion.

reproduction.observedFailure: Before the production fix, the focused scanner suite failed because cross-book recursive activation omitted entry-b, and a budget-skipped frontier still activated entry-recursive-only.
reproduction.duplicatePreflight: No existing issue PR or local issue branch found before worktree creation.

verification.targetedProofResult: Passed after review fixes. Regression rows cover cross-lorebook recursion, non-recursive lorebook frontier blocking, preventRecursion, configured recursion depth, budget-selected frontier behavior, and sticky budget exhaustion.
verification.baselineEvidence: Passed pnpm check after resolving upstream/refactor conflicts and Bunny follow-up: line endings, architecture, frontend/typecheck, Rust cargo check, docs, discovery metadata, launcher safety, and unused-code checks.
verification.visualProof: Backend logic proof: targeted Vitest output captured in command log; no meaningful UI state.
verification.focusedProof: pnpm test src/engine/generation/active-lorebook-scanner.test.ts src/engine/generation/prompt-assembly.test.ts src/engine/generation/start-generation.test.ts src/engine/generation-core/lorebooks/prompt-injector.test.ts passed after the latest conflict resolution and Bunny follow-up with 26 tests.

notes: Resolved upstream/refactor merge conflict by keeping issue-2185 verification in the singleton tracked ledger; upstream issue-2170 proof belongs to PR #2246.
Conflict-resolution focused checks passed: active-lorebook-scanner.test.ts, prompt-injector.test.ts, and start-generation.test.ts.
Bunny follow-up fixed regenerate metadata preservation so a direct target-message storage read failure aborts instead of silently dropping existing extra.
Resolved the later upstream/refactor conflict by preserving upstream lorebook included-position filtering inside the combined cross-lorebook scanner.

riskClaimMatrix.coreClaim: Recursive lorebook scanning activates entries across the full active lorebook set from budget-selected frontier content while respecting depth and preventRecursion.
positiveRowsTested: Cross-lorebook A-to-B activation from selected frontier content.
Same-book recursive-only entry remains inactive when its frontier entry exceeds the chat lorebook budget.
Later lower-priority entries remain skipped after the chat lorebook budget is exhausted.
negativeRowsTested: Entry with preventRecursion did not activate a target keyed only by that prevented content.
Configured maxRecursionDepth=1 did not activate the second-hop target keyed by first recursive result content.
A selected entry from a non-recursive lorebook did not become recursive frontier content.
adjacentRowsTested: Regenerated message metadata preservation aborts without patching when direct target lookup fails.

groundTruthFacts.fact: Legacy scanned all relevant entries together and recursed only from budget-selected candidates.
The refactor prompt-time scanner is React-free src/engine logic used by shared prompt assembly.
groundTruthFacts.method: derived from source inspection of src/engine/generation/active-lorebook-scanner.ts and prompt-assembly call sites

dependencies.evidence: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile completed in the issue worktree and did not change package.json or pnpm-lock.yaml.

finalDoneGate.notes: Focused tests, proof gate, and pnpm check passed after upstream/refactor conflict resolutions and Bunny follow-up. PR acceptance is pending push, CI, Bunny, and CodeRabbit.

reviewThreads.finding: Bunny: non-recursive lorebooks can seed recursion.
reviewThreads.reason: Active lorebook metadata now carries recursiveScanning and recursive frontier content is filtered to selected entries whose own lorebook enabled recursion. Added a regression row proving a non-recursive lorebook entry does not seed the next recursive hop.

reviewThreads.finding: Bunny: budget skip no longer exhausts lower-priority entries.
reviewThreads.reason: Budget selection state now keeps chat budget exhaustion and per-lorebook budget exhaustion sticky for later candidates. Added a regression row proving a later lower-priority entry remains skipped after the chat budget is exhausted.

reviewThreads.finding: Bunny: storage failure is transmuted into metadata loss.
reviewThreads.reason: regenerationTargetExtra now lets the direct target-message load fail instead of treating it as absent metadata. Added a regression row proving no regenerated message extra patch is written after the storage read fails.

bunny.evidence: Issue match is direct; conflict resolution kept issue-2185 proof while merging upstream/refactor changes; combined active-entry recursion now scans across active lorebooks and only recurses from budget-selected entries whose lorebook enables recursion; upstream included-position filtering is preserved before combined scanning; focused generation tests passed after conflict resolution and Bunny follow-up; no dependencies, schema, storage persistence, API, UI, auth, or release files changed.
